# CANDIDATE: BEARISH_REVERSAL_BODY_WICK_FILTER

**Filed:** 2026-06-17  
**Filer:** chef-nemotron (free-tier autonomous R&D)  
**Type:** filter_change  
**Status:** DRAFT (NEEDS-RATIFICATION per Rule 9)

## Hypothesis

BEARISH_REVERSAL LEVEL trades fail in-sample (IS) when the rejection candle has a small wick relative to body (weak rejection), but succeed out-of-sample (OOS) when the rejection candle has a large wick relative to body (strong rejection). This binary feature captures rejection strength without overfitting to VIX regimes or time-of-day.

## Mechanism

At LEVEL entry bar (level_rejection trigger), compute:
- Body size = |close - open|
- Wick size = high - low - body size (total range minus body)
Entry is allowed only if body size < wick size (i.e., wick > 50% of total range). This ensures the candle shows strong rejection of the level with indecisive close, matching the playbook's "rejection candle" definition.

## OP-20 disclosures

1. **Account-size assumption:** qty=28 requires $25K+; $1K paper ~= 14% headline
2. **Sample bias:** IS n=33, OOS n=4 (extremely small OOS sample; high overfit risk; selection method: regime split by VIX concentration)
3. **Out-of-sample:** NEEDS-OOS (OOS window is May-26 post-Liberation-Day only; needs walk-forward held-out)
4. **Real-fills:** NEEDS-REAL-FILLS (no real-fills check on top 3 J days)
5. **Failure modes:** Worst day: stop-out on weak rejection candle; max drawdown: consecutive weak-rejection losses; blow-up scenario: mistaking weak rejection for strong entry during low-VIX chop
6. **Concentration:** IS drag concentrated in VIX 15-17 (Jan-26) and VIX 25-35 (Mar-26) = ~64% of IS losses (21/33 trades); OOS winners in VIX 17-20 (May-26) = 100% of OOS wins (4/4 trades)

## Pre-merge gate

<what tests need to pass: gym validators, walk-forward, real-fills>
- Gym: BEARISH_REVERSAL watcher must pass all offline tests
- Walk-forward: OOS/IS Sharpe ratio >= 0.70 on BEARISH_REVERSAL LEVEL trades with filter
- Real-fills: top 3 J days must show P&L improvement vs baseline with realistic OPRA fills

3 / 10 -- <brief reasoning>
